meaning: n.急速活动,挤,推,拥挤喧嚷vt.驱赶,驱逐,乱挤,硬逼(某人)买或做事,强夺vi.硬挤过去
examples: You'll have to hustle if you're to get home for supper.
I like it here. It forces you to hustle and you can earn money.
We escaped from the hustle and bustle of the city for the weekend.
He sees himself as one of them, Charlie Hustle in a city of hustlers.
With hikes like that, you really have to hustle just to limit the damage.
